records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Leaved (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Leaving]

defs: To send out leaves; to leaf; -- often with out.

records.ety: [OE. leve, leave, AS. lef; akin to lef pleasing, dear, E. lief, D. oorlof leave, G. arlaub, and erlauben to permit, Icel. leyfi. √124. See Lief.]

defs: Liberty granted by which restraint or illegality is removed; permission; allowance; license.
quotes: David earnestly asked leave of me.
No friend has leave to bear away the dead.

defs: The act of leaving or departing; a formal parting; a leaving; farewell; adieu; -- used chiefly in the phrase, to take leave, i. e., literally, to take permission to go.
quotes: A double blessing is a'double grace; Occasion smiles upon a second leave.
And Paul after this tarried there yet a good while, and then took his leave of the brethren.

records.ety: [OE. leven, AS. l<?/fan, fr. lāf remnant, heritage; akin to lifian, libban, to live, orig., to remain; cf. belīfan to remain, G. bleiben, Goth. bileiban. √119. See Live, v.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Left (lft); p. pr. & vb. n. Leaving.]

defs: To withdraw one's self from; to go away from; to depart from; as, to leave the house.
quotes: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife.

defs: To let remain unremoved or undone; to let stay or continue, in distinction from what is removed or changed.
quotes: If grape gatherers come to thee, would they not leave some gleaning grapes ?
These ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.
Besides it leaveth a suspicion, as if more might be said than is expressed.

defs: To cease from; to desist from; to abstain from.
quotes: Now leave complaining and begin your tea.

defs: To desert; to abandon; to forsake; hence, to give up; to relinquish.
quotes: Lo, we have left all, and have followed thee.
The heresies that men do leave.

defs: To let be or do without interference; as, I left him to his reflections; I leave my hearers to judge.
quotes: I will leave you now to your gossiplike humor.

defs: To put; to place; to deposit; to deliver; to commit; to submit -- with a sense of withdrawing one's self from; as, leave your hat in the hall; we left our cards; to leave the matter to arbitrators.
quotes: Leave there thy gift before the altar and go thy way.
The foot That leaves the print of blood where'er it walks.

defs: To have remaining at death; hence, to bequeath; as, he left a large estate; he left a good name; he left a legacy to his niece.

to cause to be; -- followed by an adjective or adverb describing a state or condition; as, the losses due to fire leave me penniless; The cost of defending himself left Bill Clinton with a mountain of lawyers' bills.

quotes: By the time I left for Scotland.

defs: To cease; to desist; to leave off.
quotes: Leave off, and for another summons wait.
